name: CERT: Priority and Level Assigned
description: Each finding has a Priority (Severity × Likelihood × Remediation Cost, 1–27) and resulting Level (L1/L2/L3) assigned.
rationale: SEI CERT: the three risk factors multiply to a Priority (1–27), which maps to a Level — L1 (Priority 12–27), L2 (6–9), L3 (1–4).
source: SEI CERT Coding Standards (Carnegie Mellon Software Engineering Institute) — CERT C and CERT C++ Secure Coding Standards, with the CERT risk-assessment methodology (Severity × Likelihood × Remediation Cost → Priority → Level L1/L2/L3). https://wiki.sei.cmu.edu/confluence/display/seccode
